situational_context: Active rebuild phase with return to running initiated today (first run in 182 days: 2.5 km Z2, conservative). Chronic load rising from near-zero baseline (23.7, up from 17.8 at bootstrap). Body weight 104.8 kg (+20 kg vs peak running era) creates elevated musculoskeletal risk per return-to-running protocol. ACWR normalized to 1.06 after May 1 gravel spike (1.49). Vitamin D insufficient (27 ng/mL, Dec 2025) remains a stress fracture risk factor. Young baby (Léonie, 4 months) — expect occasional training disruptions, missed sessions are normal. Cycling remains primary modality; running being layered in cautiously with minimum 48h between sessions and strict tissue-feedback gating.

plan_7d_ahead.type: Repos ou marche légère — absorption charge semaine
Endurance Z2 vélo ou run court si tissus OK

recommendations.text: Follow return-to-running protocol strictly: no second run before Saturday May 9 at earliest, only if zero residual pain (Achilles, patellar, tibia, plantar) at 48h. If any localized pain persists >24h post-run, skip running next week entirely and substitute cycling.
recommendations.measurable: Zero localized musculoskeletal pain 48h after today's run; maximum 1 additional run this week

recommendations.text: Manage total weekly load around commute choices: if gravel commuting on sur site days (May 6-7), reduce or cancel Saturday session. Ebike commute is compatible with full plan. Target acute load <=27.5.
Increase protein intake to consistently hit 150g/day — 7d average is 127g (85% of target). Critical during return-to-running for collagen synthesis and tendon remodeling at current body weight.
recommendations.measurable: >=5 of 7 days with protein >=140g in the coming week

markers_to_watch.threshold: Zero localized pain at 48h post-run (by May 5)
markers_to_watch.reason: First run in 182 days at 105 kg — Achilles, patellar tendon, plantar fascia, tibia are high-risk; Vitamin D 27 ng/mL doubles stress fracture risk
Running adds a new impact modality on top of cycling base; gravel commutes on sur site days can spike acute load

markers_to_watch.threshold: Track trajectory — dropped 40.9 to 38.6 after first run
markers_to_watch.reason: Garmin recalibrated from frozen estimate to actual running performance; expect gradual improvement
